papers.title: An Ultrastrong and Ductile Duplex Lightweight Steel with Dual-Nanoprecipitation
papers.paper_scope: Design and investigation of an Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni duplex lightweight steel with austenite-B2 dual-phase microstructure and dual-nanoprecipitation (L'1_2 LRO domains and VC) via simple rolling and heat treatment, achieving ultrahigh strength and ductility, and elucidation of multiple strengthening and strain hardening mechanisms.

alloys.alloys_notes: Measured compositions (wt.%): Mn 30.45, Al 9.50, C 1.20, V 1.09, Ni 4.94, P 0.0040, S 0.0044, Fe Bal.

processes.description: Induction melting of 10 kg ingot under high-purity argon, homogenization at 1050°C for 1 h, hot rolling to 3 mm (~85% reduction), water quenching, cold rolling to 1.5 mm (50% total reduction, single-pass ≤5%), solution treatment at 900°C for 15 min, and final water quenching.

processing_steps.processing_steps_notes: 10 kg ingot under high-purity argon atmosphere
Rolled from 20 mm to 3 mm thickness
From 3 mm to ~1.5 mm; single-pass reduction rate maintained at or below 5%

structures.overall_structure: Duplex microstructure comprising austenite and B2 phases with dual-nanoprecipitation of L'1_2 type long-range ordered domains and VC carbides within austenite

phases_present.morphology: long strip-like large grains and fine particles decorating austenite grain boundaries

defects.dislocation_density: Residual dislocations from incomplete annihilation during 900°C annealing; initial GND density ~5.36×10^13 m^-2 in austenite and ~1.04×10^14 m^-2 in B2

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: Austenite shows unimodal distribution; B2 exhibits bimodal distribution with mean sizes of 1.02 µm and 3.38 µm

defect_interaction.interaction_type: dislocation pile-up and GND accumulation at heterogeneous interfaces
defect_interaction.quality_description: Accumulation of geometrically necessary dislocations at austenite/B2 boundaries generates long-range back stresses in soft regions and forward stresses in hard regions, producing HDI strengthening and work-hardening

interface_notes.stress_strain_distribution: Rapid relaxation of internal stress and strain at austenite/B2 interface after yielding of hard B2 phase; pronounced strain gradients develop during deformation due to strain incompatibility between phases and grains

interface_notes.slippage_situation: Austenite/B2 interface impedes planar dislocation slip and reflects incoming dislocations, leading to formation of new planar dislocation arrays in the austenitic matrix near phase boundaries

interfaces.phase_evolution: During deformation, dislocations shear through L'1_2 LRO domains causing partial destruction and reduced volume fraction; VC particles impede dislocation motion with hindering effect becoming more pronounced at higher strains; dislocation density increases significantly in both austenite and B2 phases with straining

characterization_methods.purpose: Phase identification and volume fraction quantification
characterization_methods.key_findings: Diffraction peaks corresponding to austenite, B2, and VC identified; B2 phase volume fraction quantified as 20% by direct comparison method

characterization_methods.purpose: Microstructure characterization, grain size measurement, GND density mapping, and Schmid factor analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Duplex microstructure with equiaxed austenite (avg 1.31 µm) and bimodal B2 grains; GND densities increase monotonically in both phases during deformation; Schmid factor differences govern individual grain deformation behavior

characterization_methods.purpose: Nanoscale microstructure, precipitate morphology, interface coherency, and chemical composition analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: VC average size 19.9±6.2 nm with 0.26% volume fraction and coherent interface with austenite; L'1_2 LRO domains average 1.0±0.3 nm with 11.10% volume fraction and irregular shapes; B2/austenite interface is semi-coherent

Co-deformation of austenite and B2 phases; GND density increases from 5.36×10^13 to 2.64×10^14 m^-2 in austenite and from 1.04×10^14 to 2.51×10^14 m^-2 in B2 up to 6% strain; dislocation pile-ups observed near B2 phase
Yield strength 1316±16 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 1458±11 MPa, and total elongation 11.7±1.2% at strain rate of 1×10^-3 s^-1; ductile dimple fracture morphology observed

characterization_methods.purpose: HDI stress evaluation and Bauschinger effect analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Hysteresis area and yield drop increase with strain; HDI stress increases progressively from ~620 MPa at 1% true strain to ~845 MPa at 11% true strain; HDI contribution to YS determined as 597 MPa by extrapolation

computational_details.simulation_method: Thermodynamic equilibrium calculation and analytical strengthening modeling
computational_details.validation_against_experiment: Equilibrium phase diagram predictions consistent with XRD and EBSD observed phase fractions; analytical model calculations of yield strength compared against experimental YS

analytical_model.equation_role: Overall YS from rule of mixtures with HDI term
analytical_model.parameters: σ_YS = σ_γ f_γ + σ_B2 f_B2 + σ_HDI
analytical_model.model_note: σ_HDI obtained by extrapolation from LUR curve equals 597 MPa

analytical_model.equation_role: Sum of strengthening contributions in austenite
analytical_model.parameters: σ_γ = σ_0,γ + σ_ss,γ + σ_dis,γ + σ_VC,γ + σ_LRO,γ
analytical_model.model_note: σ_0,γ = 97 MPa; σ_ss,γ = 228 MPa; σ_dis,γ = 106 MPa; σ_VC,γ = 92 MPa; σ_LRO,γ = 119 MPa by difference

analytical_model.equation_role: Sum of strengthening contributions in B2 phase
Calculate solid solution strengthening in austenite from composition
analytical_model.model_note: Calculated value 228 MPa using mass fractions of alloying elements
Calculated contribution 92 MPa based on VC fraction and average diameter

unmapped_findings: The equilibrium phase diagram calculated by Thermo-Calc 2021b indicates phase fractions at 900°C of approximately 78% austenite, 20% B2, 1% VC, and 0% κ-carbide.
The fracture surface of the tensile specimen is characterized by dimples, indicating a ductile fracture mode.

Work-hardening rate behavior exhibits three discernible stages: stage I (0–1%) sharp decrease to below zero; stage II (1–4.3%) rapid increase to a maximum; stage III (>4.3%) gradual decrease while remaining at a relatively high level of 2.7 to 3.7 GPa.
LUR test detailed evolution: hysteresis area increases from 70 MPa at 1% true strain to 202 MPa at 11% true strain; yield drop Δσy increases from 2 MPa to 23 MPa over the same range.

GND density evolution during tensile deformation: austenite increases from 5.36×10^13 m^-2 at 0% strain to 2.64×10^14 m^-2 at 6% strain; B2 increases from 1.04×10^14 m^-2 at 0% strain to 2.51×10^14 m^-2 at 6% strain.

During deformation, L'1_2 LRO domains within austenite slip bands are sheared by dislocations, leading to weakened superlattice reflections and reduced volume fraction; conversely, VC particles impede dislocation motion and cause dislocation accumulation around them.

Calculated weighted strengthening contributions summarized in Figure 9: σ_HDI 597 MPa; σ_0,γ f_γ 78 MPa; σ_ss,γ f_γ 182 MPa; σ_dis,γ f_γ 85 MPa; σ_VC f_γ 74 MPa; σ_LRO f_γ 119 MPa; σ_0,B2 f_B2 135 MPa; σ_dis,B2 f_B2 46 MPa.

The B2 phase exhibits a higher hardness than ferrite, and the hardness mismatch between austenite and B2 phases is regulated by precipitation behavior within austenite to balance strength-ductility synergy.
